title: Demystifying the .NET Ecosystem Webinar
headline: .NET Framework, .NET Core, .NET and C# versions, SDKs, Visual Studio versions - Finally Make Sense of the Whole Ecosystem
subheadline: A free live session where the entire .NET ecosystem is explained with clear visuals and real examples - so the versions, runtimes and frameworks finally click into place.
painPoints: You keep seeing .NET Framework, .NET Core and just ".NET" everywhere and you're never quite sure which is which
Is C# and .NET the same thing? And how do Visual Studio versions, .NET SDKs and runtime versions all fit together? It's a mess in your head
You're not sure whether to start a new project on .NET Framework or modern .NET (and which version to use) - and the decision keeps haunting you
Terms like CLR, IL, JIT, BCL and LTS float around in conversations but you've never had them explained simply
Which C# version is released in which .NET version? And what about ASP.NET Core, .NET MAUI, EF Core versions? It's overwhelming and you just want a clear picture of how it all fits together
transformation: Clearly tell apart .NET Framework, .NET Core and modern .NET - and know which one fits your situation
Understand at a high level how C# code becomes IL, gets loaded by the CLR and runs as machine code through JIT
Build a clear mental model of how C#, .NET SDKs, Visual Studio, runtime versions and libraries fit together
Get clear on how to follow new version features and releases - so you can plan your learning and projects with confidence instead of guesswork
whatYouWillDo: Watch a clear overview of how a C# app is compiled into Common Intermediate Language and run by the CLR
See the differences between .NET Framework, .NET Core and modern .NET explained side by side with real context
Follow an explanation of Long Term Support (LTS) vs Standard Term Support (STS) releases and the yearly release cadence - so version planning stops feeling like guesswork
Listen in on a C# version history overview, with a peek at some latest features through a demo
prerequisites: Some familiarity with programming is helpful, but no .NET expertise is required. This webinar is built to be approachable - just bring your curiosity.
whyDifferent: The ecosystem is explained live and visually - not as abstract theory you'll forget by tomorrow
Real-world guidance on when to use which .NET version - backed by practical scenarios
Support windows, end-of-life and why enterprises prefer LTS are explained in plain language
Recordings remain available for a period so you can cross-check and revisit any explanation at your own pace
Designed so the concepts actually stick
Live Q&A and feedback segment included so your specific questions get answered on the spot

instructor.name: Mehfooz Kausmally
instructor.description: A seasoned software professional from Mauritius with over a decade of experience in the IT industry. After a successful career as a developer in multinational companies working extensively with .NET, C# and backend systems, Mehfooz transitioned into full-time professional coaching. He has already guided over 270 engineers across different technologies and levels - bringing real-world expertise and a clear, practical approach into every session.
